steps.title: Use the graph values
steps.content: The example must use the Amur grape data to show that opposite-side orientation is much more common than same-side orientation. The graph shows about 150 opposite-side pairs and about 60 same-side pairs.

steps.title: Reject inaccurate counts
steps.content: B misreads the same-side count as about 49, C says there were no same-side pairs, and D reverses the two counts. The choice with about 150 opposite-side pairs and about 60 same-side pairs matches the graph. The answer is A.
